title: Aftermath - Let's Play - Part 5
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=syPu5HdMT4k

The final map of the *Raid on Enjo* mission: the treat locker at the bottom of the vending machine. Daniel (@dungeondive) and his battered critters drop in to find a hoard of cheese prizes, the missing Snoodles, and a thoroughly unwell rat named **Red-Eye Wee'rd** — Aftermath's first boss, a blighted survivor of the great calamity with rusty-metal claws crudely lashed to his paws and a small swarm of pet roaches.

Red-Eye's defence of 8 means most attacks will fail outright. Mesaiya gets unusually lucky on two dice rolls in a row and lands both hits required; Red-Eye doesn't die so much as *limp away*, hauled off by his loyal roaches as the heroes recoil. The boss drops a broken-but-promising **action figure vest** (+2 defence in melee, with a strength-test reroll) — the first real piece of repairable kit the campaign has produced. The discovery deck delivers **mission card 5: The Blighted** for future play.

The chapter's genuine anticlimax is the roach cleanup. Six 1-HP enemies, each killable only one-at-a-time per attack, no real loot once they're flagged as Hunt-spawned. Daniel calls it openly boring and floats a house rule: spillover damage on attack-rolls past a roach's defence should kill more than one.

----

Daniel's complaint about the roach swarm — six 1-HP enemies, one kill per attack, no spillover — is that low-HP swarms become attrition rather than tactics. What's the cleanest fix for that without nerfing the *threat* of a swarm?
